feed.title: Notice: Can BIP52 be moved to Closed?

entry.title: Notice: Can BIP52 be moved to Closed?
entry.summary:
The ongoing review of Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) has highlighted the need to evaluate the progress of various proposals currently in Draft status. One such proposal, BIP52 titled "Durable, Low Energy Bitcoin PoW," appears to have been stagnant for several years without any significant advancement towards completion or active public discussions. This situation prompts a reevaluation of its status.
According to the guidelines specified in [BIP3](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0003.mddraft--closed), a BIP can be voluntarily moved by its authors from Draft to Closed if it fails to make progress, remains unaddressed despite accumulated feedback, or seems otherwise stalled for more than a year. Given these conditions and the apparent lack of momentum on BIP52, there is a proposal to update its status to Closed unless the authors respond within four weeks with intentions to continue their work on the proposal. If the authors are indeed still aiming to pursue this BIP, there exists an opportunity to advance its status to Complete, contingent on further developments and discussions that may take place on platforms such as [bips2165](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2165).
This move to potentially close BIP52 underscores the importance of maintaining active engagement and progress in the development and implementation phases of BIPs to ensure they remain relevant and actionable within the Bitcoin development community.
